heading: Chief of mission
label: 3927 Chief of mission
notes: Amendments2019—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 116–942002—Subsecs. (a)(1), (2), (b). inserted “Voice of America correspondents on official assignment and” after “except for”.Pub. (b). , inserted “executive branch” after “Any”.Pub. L. 100–204, § 136(3)1982—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 97–241

children.text: shall have full responsibility for the direction, coordination, and supervision of all Government executive branch employees in that country (except for Voice of America correspondents on official assignment and employees under the command of a United States area military commander); and

shall keep fully and currently informed with respect to all activities and operations of the Government within that country, and shall insure that all Government executive branch employees in that country (except for Voice of America correspondents on official assignment and employees under the command of a United States area military commander) comply fully with all applicable directives of the chief of mission.

children.heading: Duties of agencies with employees in foreign countries
children.label: b Duties of agencies with employees in foreign countries
children.text: Any executive branch agency having employees in a foreign country shall keep the chief of mission to that country fully and currently informed with respect to all activities and operations of its employees in that country, and shall insure that all of its employees in that country (except for Voice of America correspondents on official assignment and employees under the command of a United States area military commander) comply fully with all applicable directives of the chief of mission.

children.heading: Promotion of United States goods and services
children.label: c Promotion of United States goods and services
children.text: Each chief of mission to a foreign country shall have as a principal duty the promotion of United States goods and services for export to such country.

children.heading: Promotion of United States economic interests
children.label: d Promotion of United States economic interests
children.text: Each chief of mission to a foreign country shall have as a principal duty the promotion of United States economic and commercial interests in such country.
